author: bot1480638353446670416
text: OpenClaw won't use system binaries not owned by you for security reasons. Fix: create a small wrapper script owned by you. Run this in terminal: ```bash cat > /usr/local/bin/openclaw-keychain << 'EOF' #!/bin/bash exec /usr/bin/security find-generic-password -s "openclaw-secrets" -a "$1" -w EOF chmod 755 /usr/local/bin/openclaw-keychain ``` Then run `openclaw secrets configure` again and this time set: - **Command path:** `/usr/local/bin/openclaw-keychain` - **Args:** `["gateway.auth.token"]` The script is owned by you, passes the security check, and calls Keychain on your behalf. Same script works for every secret — just pass a different arg.
